title: chrysocoma cernua
intro: Chrysocoma cernua, also known as the bitter bush or bitter-herb, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the coastal regions of South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its bitter properties, which are said to have medicinal properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. Chrysocoma cernua is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, with the leaves and stems being used to make a bitter herbal remedy that is said to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
